sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
After loading your data or [Load Sample Data Into Atlas](sample-data), you can
use the Atlas UI to interact with the data in the following ways:

- [Atlas UI](atlas-ui-dbs) in your
  clusters.

- [check](atlas-ui-collections) in your
  clusters.

- [Create, View, Update, and Delete Documents](atlas-ui-docs) in your collections.

- [Atlas UI](atlas-ui-indexes) on your collections.

- [Aggregation Pipeline Builder](atlas-ui-agg-pipeline) to
  process data in your collections.

- [Atlas UI](de-shard-collection-for-global-writes)
  to distribute large datasets evenly.

- [Build charts](charts/launch-charts) to visualize data in your
  databases and collections.

sections.heading: Required Roles
sections.body:
The following table describes the roles required to perform various actions on
an Atlas cluster:

| Action                       | Required Roles                                             |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| Create Databases             | At least the Project Data Access Read/Write role.          |
| View Databases               | At least the Project Data Access Read Only role.           |
| Drop Databases               | At least the Project Data Access Admin role.               |
| Create Collections           | At least the Project Data Access Read/Write role.          |
| View Collections             | At least the Project Data Access Read Only role.           |
| Drop Collections             | At least the Project Data Access Admin role.               |
| Shard Collections            | One of the following roles:Organization OwnerProject Owner |
| Insert Documents             | At least the Project Data Access Read/Write role.          |
| Delete Documents             | At least the Project Data Access Read/Write role.          |
| Edit Documents               | At least the Project Data Access Read/Write role.          |
| Create Indexes               | At least the Project Data Access Admin role.               |
| Drop Indexes                 | At least the Project Data Access Admin role.               |
| Hide Indexes                 | At least the Project Data Access Admin role.               |
| View Indexes                 | At least the Project Data Access Read Only role.           |
| Create Aggregation Pipelines | At least the Project Data Access Read/Write role.          |

sections.heading: Atlas UI Read Behavior
sections.body:
The Atlas UI reads from the primary unless the primary is
unavailable. If the primary is unavailable, the Atlas UI
reads from a non-hidden, non-delayed secondary member.

sections.heading: Disable Atlas UI Data Interaction
sections.body:
To interact with your data in the Atlas UI as described in the
[Overview section](atlas-ui-overview), the **Data
Explorer** needs to be enabled. Disabling the **Data Explorer**
will not prevent users from building [MongoDB Charts](charts) in the
Atlas UI.

> **Important:** 
>
> Required Privileges
>
> To enable or disable **Data Explorer** for a project, you
> must have the [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)  role for the project
> or the [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role on its parent organization.

**Data Explorer** is enabled by default. To disable
**Data Explorer**:

> **Important:** 
>
> When Data Explorer is disabled, you cannot:
>
> - Terminate slow operations from the
>   [Real-Time Performance Panel](real-time-metrics-status-tab).
>
> - Create indexes from the
>   [Performance Advisor](performance-advisor). You
>   can still view Performance Advisor recommendations, but you must
>   create those indexes from [mongosh](mongodb-shell).
>
> - Use the [Search Tester](atlas-search-queries) to run Search queries.
>   You can still run Search queries using [mongosh](mongodb-shell), [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), or
>   [MongoDB drivers](drivers).

To enable **Data Explorer**, set the toggle to **On**.

sections.heading: Troubleshoot
sections.body:
If you are experiencing issues connecting to your cluster on
**Data Explorer**, see [Troubleshoot Data Explorer Issues](atlas-ui-troubleshoot).
